author: Dr. Sarah Chen
hpi:
Mr. Morrison is a 71-year-old male with HFrEF (EF 30-35%), persistent atrial fibrillation on warfarin, CKD Stage 3b, Type 2 DM on insulin and metformin, diabetic peripheral neuropathy, HTN, hyperlipidemia, BPH, and a history of UTI (April 2023, resolved) who presents for comprehensive primary care follow-up. He has not been seen in our office since May and has had several subspecialty visits in the interim. This is the first of a series of focused visits this week to address his multiple chronic conditions comprehensively.

He reports that overall he is feeling about the same as his last visit. His exercise tolerance remains stable at approximately 1-1.5 blocks before he becomes dyspneic. He denies any acute symptoms — no chest pain, no worsening dyspnea, no fevers, no falls. He states his blood sugars have improved since his insulin was increased to 26 units. He had a UTI in April that resolved with nitrofurantoin, and he has had no recurrence of urinary symptoms. He notes some baseline dyspnea with exertion and bilateral knee pain that is managed with acetaminophen and topical diclofenac.

He had a V/Q scan in May that was low probability for PE per pulmonology. His dyspnea is attributed to his heart failure and deconditioning. Cardiology has been uptitrating his carvedilol — he is currently on 18.75mg BID (up from 12.5mg earlier this year), tolerating well with no symptomatic bradycardia or hypotension. He brings a home blood pressure log showing readings generally 125-145/72-88.

Socially, he and his wife Patricia are doing well. They recently celebrated their 48th wedding anniversary. He is still active in his church community though attending less frequently due to fatigue. He denies depression but acknowledges he has less energy and motivation than he used to.

reviewOfSystems.Constitutional: Chronic fatigue as above, no fevers, no chills, no unintentional weight loss. Sleep quality fair — disrupted by nocturia and nocturnal neuropathy
reviewOfSystems.Cardiovascular: DOE at baseline (1-1.5 blocks), no orthopnea, no PND, no chest pain, no palpitations, no syncope
reviewOfSystems.Respiratory: No cough, no wheezing, no hemoptysis. Dyspnea exertional only
reviewOfSystems.GI: Appetite fair, no abdominal pain, no nausea/vomiting, no melena, no hematochezia, regular bowel movements
reviewOfSystems.GU: Nocturia x2-3 (BPH), no dysuria, no hematuria. UTI resolved
reviewOfSystems.Musculoskeletal: Bilateral knee OA pain 3-4/10, managed with acetaminophen and topical diclofenac. No acute joint swelling
reviewOfSystems.Neurologic: Bilateral foot numbness and tingling, worse at night. No new weakness. No falls. Occasional clumsiness
reviewOfSystems.Endocrine: Blood sugars improved, no hypoglycemia, no polyuria beyond baseline nocturia
reviewOfSystems.Skin: Dry skin lower extremities, no foot ulcers, no new rashes
reviewOfSystems.Psychiatric: Low energy and decreased motivation but denies frank depression. Enjoys time with grandchildren when they visit

physicalExam.General: Well-appearing obese male, alert and oriented, no acute distress. Affect pleasant but subdued. BMI approximately 33
physicalExam.HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. PERRL. Sclera anicteric. Conjunctivae with mild pallor (chronic). Oropharynx clear. Mild gingivitis. No thyromegaly. No cervical lymphadenopathy
physicalExam.Cardiovascular: Irregularly irregular at 69 bpm. S1/S2 normal. 2/6 holosystolic murmur at apex (stable). No S3 or S4. JVP approximately 7 cm H2O — normal. PMI laterally displaced. No carotid bruits
physicalExam.Respiratory: Trace fine crackles bilateral bases posteriorly, clearing with deep cough. No wheezes or rhonchi. Good air entry bilaterally
physicalExam.Abdomen: Obese, soft, non-tender, non-distended. Normal active bowel sounds. No hepatosplenomegaly. Small reducible umbilical hernia, stable
physicalExam.Extremities: 1+ bilateral pitting edema at ankles. Warm, well-perfused. Dry skin bilateral lower legs with mild scaling. No ulcers. No venous stasis changes. Dorsalis pedis pulses 1+ bilaterally
physicalExam.Neurologic: Alert and oriented x3. Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Decreased sensation bilateral feet in stocking distribution. Ankle reflexes absent. Romberg mildly positive. Gait slightly wide-based but ambulatory without assistive device
physicalExam.Psychiatric: Flat affect noted. Makes eye contact. Speech normal rate and rhythm. Thought process linear. No suicidal ideation expressed

assessment.notes: Complex patient with 10+ active diagnoses requiring coordinated management across 5+ subspecialties. Current disease state summary: HFrEF NYHA II (stable), A-fib (rate controlled on warfarin), CKD 3b (eGFR ~37, stable decline), DM2 (improving on increased insulin), neuropathy (moderate, progressing), HTN (near goal), hyperlipidemia (at goal), BPH (moderate symptoms on tamsulosin). No acute issues today. Focus this week on comprehensive optimization.

NYHA Class II, stable. On Entresto (target), carvedilol (uptitrating, now 18.75mg BID), spironolactone, furosemide. No SGLT2 inhibitor yet. Volume status: trace overload (1+ edema, minimal crackles) but clinically acceptable.
Glycemic control improving on glargine 26 units + metformin 250mg BID. A1c to be rechecked. Complications: neuropathy (progressing), nephropathy (CKD 3b), retinopathy (mild NPDR).

Flat affect, decreased energy and motivation, reduced social engagement. Not meeting full criteria for major depression per screening today (PHQ-2: 2/6 — subthreshold). However, chronic disease burden and functional decline are significant psychosocial stressors. Worth monitoring closely.

Several preventive care items are due or overdue: annual flu shot (due fall 2023), pneumococcal vaccination (Prevnar 20 given 9/2022 — complete), COVID booster (last 10/2022 — may need update), colon cancer screening (colonoscopy pending scheduling via GI), annual diabetic eye exam (completed, mild NPDR), annual podiatry exam (scheduled next month).

plan.action:
Plan multi-day visit series this week to address each chronic condition in dedicated time:
- Day 2 (7/6): UTI follow-up and medication reconciliation
- Day 3 (7/7): Diabetes management focused visit
- Day 4 (7/8): Dyspnea/SOB evaluation and cardiac rehab planning
- Day 5 (7/9): CKD management and lab review

Active subspecialty teams: Cardiology (Dr. Wilson), GI (Dr. Thompson), Pulmonology (Dr. Park), Urology (Dr. Kim), Internal Medicine (Dr. Adams), Nephrology (established). Communication sent to all teams via chart update regarding this comprehensive reassessment.

Continue current GDMT. Cardiology managing carvedilol uptitration. Will address in detail at SOB-focused visit (7/8). Cardiac rehab referral to be facilitated. Volume status acceptable — no change to diuretics today.

Will address in detail at diabetes-focused visit (7/7). Labs ordered today: A1c, BMP, CBC with differential, lipid panel, LFTs, phosphorus, PTH, vitamin D, UACR, urinalysis, uric acid, iron studies, ferritin, TIBC, TSH.

PHQ-2 score 2/6 today — subthreshold but concerning given flat affect and decreased social engagement. Will administer full PHQ-9 at next comprehensive visit. Discussed with patient — he attributes his low energy to his heart condition and multiple medications. Discussed that depression is common in patients with chronic HF and can worsen outcomes. He is open to counseling if recommended but skeptical about 'needing it.' Will continue to monitor. If PHQ-9 elevated, will discuss counseling and/or SSRI options (sertraline preferred in cardiac patients).

Influenza vaccine — to be given at fall visit. COVID booster — will discuss updated formulation when available. Colonoscopy — being scheduled by GI. Podiatry — appointment confirmed for August. Ophthalmology — completed, follow-up per their schedule.
Continue visit series this week. Next comprehensive PCP visit in 6-8 weeks after all subspecialty inputs integrated. Emergency precautions reviewed with patient and wife.

attestation:
I personally saw and examined the patient. I agree with the documented findings and plan.

Electronically signed by: Dr. Sarah Chen
Date: 7/5/2023
